lines.en_lit: his service initiates him. Wasn’t it taught all the vessels that Moses made, their anointment consecrates them. From that forward, their service initiates them Here, too, his service initiates him.
When Rav Dimi came he said: the belt of the common priest, Rabbi and Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon. One said: of diverse kinds And one said: fine linen,

Conclude that it is Rabbi who said of diverse kinds, as it was taught The only between a High Priest and a common priest is belt; the statement of Rabbi Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says: Not even belt when If we say the rest of the days of the year, there are many the High Priest serves eight garments and the common four
say: No, actually, during the rest of the days of the year. those common

When Ravin came he stated the belt of the High Priest on Yom Kippur, everyone agrees of fine linen, the belt of the High Priest during the rest of the days of the year, everyone agrees of diverse kinds. They disagreed only with regard to the belt of the common priest both during the rest of the days of the year and on Yom Kippur, as Rabbi of diverse kinds, and Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, says of linen.

Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: We, too, have learned upon his flesh” what does the verse state: “Shall he put upon,” Rabbi Yehuda says: to include mitre belt for the removal of the ashes, the statement of Rabbi Yehuda.

Rabbi Dosa says to include the garments of the High Priest on Yom Kippur are fit for a common priest. Rabbi says: There are two responses to statement One: The belt of the High Priest on Yom Kippur is not the belt of the common priest
And furthermore, garments that were used extreme sanctity, will use them minor sanctity? Rather, what does the verse state Shall put on? to include threadbare
And Rabbi Dosa follows his reasoning, as it was taught and shall leave them there” teaches require interment Rabbi Dosa says: may not use them Yom Kippur a different

The Sages taught a disqualification befalls and they appointed another in his stead, original returns to his service. second, all the mitzvot of the High Priesthood are upon him. the statement of Rabbi Meir.
Rabbi Yosei says: original returns to his service, second is fit neither as High Priest nor as a common priest

Rabbi Yosei said an incident involving Yosef ben Elem of Tzippori, disqualification befell a High Priest, appointed him in his stead. the Sages said: original returns to his service, second is fit neither as High Priest nor as a common priest. High Priest, due to hatred, common priest, because One elevates in sanctity and one does not downgrade.
Rabba bar bar Ḥana said Rabbi Yoḥanan said:
